rationale: The study explicitly describes a 'quasi-natural experiment using a difference-in-differences (DID) design and a changes-in-changes (CIC) estimation approach' that emerged when 'HOTTM set Rapid (an AI tool) as the default editor for volunteers, creating an exogenous introduction of AI.' This is a clear quasi-experimental identification strategy comparing treated volunteers who adopted the AI tool to control volunteers who did not, using DID and CIC methods.
